R&DandRevenue  FiscalYear2024
Revenue(KRW)  NetIncome(KRW)  R&DExpense(KRW)  R&DtoRevenue(%)  CAPEX(KRW)
GeographicalBreakdown  Asia-Pacific  1,200,000,000  300,000,000  180,000,000
TotalR&DExpense  200,000,000  R&DtoRevenueRatio  13.33  EndofPeriod
In the fiscal year 2024, NVIDIA reported a total revenue of $1,500,000,000 and a net income of $350,000,000, with a research
and development (R&D) expense of $200,000,000, resulting in an R&D to revenue ratio of 13.33% and capital expenditures
(CAPEX) amounting to $1.2M. The GPUDivision generated $1,000,000,000 in revenue, with a net income of $250,000,000,
incurring an R&D expense of $150,000,000, leading to an R&D to revenue ratio of 15.00%. The DataCenter segment recorded
$300,000,000 in revenue and a net income of $50,000,000, allocating $30,000,000 for R&D, which corresponds to an R&D to
revenue ratio of 10.00%. Within the AutomotiveSector, revenue stood at $100,000,000 with a net income of $20,000,000 and
R&D expenses of $15,000,000, yielding an R&D to revenue ratio of 15.00%. The SoftwareServices division also reported
$100,000,000 in revenue and a net income of $30,000,000, with an R&D expense of $5,000,000, reflecting an R&D to revenue
ratio of 5.00%. In terms of geographical breakdown, Asia-Pacific contributed $1,200,000,000 in revenue, $300,000,000 in net
income, and $180,000,000 in R&D expenses. North America generated $250,000,000 in revenue, $50,000,000 in net income,
and allocated $20,000,000 for R&D, corresponding to an R&D to revenue ratio of 8.00%. Europe contributed $50,000,000 in
revenue with no net income or R&D expenses. The total R&D expense for the fiscal year amounted to $200,000,000,
culminating in an end-of-period R&D to revenue ratio of 13.33%.
